input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Header>
        <RefreshView
            Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
            IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
        </RefreshView>
    </CollectionView.Header>

    <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
            <Grid Padding="10">
                <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
            </Grid>
        </DataTemplate>
    </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
</CollectionView>
```

A simpler and more common pattern — wrap the whole `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView`:

```xml
<RefreshView Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
             IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">
    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionMode="Single"
        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class MyViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IMyApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        IsRefreshing = true;
        var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
        Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
        IsRefreshing = false;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task ItemSelected()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;
        await _nav.NavigateToDetailAsync(SelectedItem.Id);
        SelectedItem = null; // reset so user can re-select
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` → `Items` | Drives the list from an `ObservableCollection` |
| `SelectedItem` → `SelectedItem` (TwoWay) | Tracks which item is tapped |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` → `ItemSelectedCommand` | Fires navigation on tap |
| `RefreshView.Command` → `RefreshCommand` | Triggers API reload on pull |
| `IsRefreshing` → `IsRefreshing` | Controls the refresh spinner |

**Tips:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so the same row can be tapped again.
- Wrapping `CollectionView` inside `RefreshView` is the standard MAUI pattern for pull-to-refresh.
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`).
